title: spergula pycnorrhiza
intro: Spergula pycnorrhiza, also known as corn spurry or corn cleaver, is a type of annual weed native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. It has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as digestive problems like gastritis and diarrhea. The herbal preparations of spergula pycnorrhiza include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used internally to promote health and well-being.
